Women Are Too Controlling, She Wrote. Her Message Still Resonates.

I cover cultural and political shifts affecting women in America. When I learned that women who embrace traditional gender roles and those who don’t are both reading Laura Doyle’s book, I wanted to know more.

The premise of Doyle’s “The Surrendered Wife,” published a quarter-century ago, is that women hold the keys to their relationships and that they need to avoid being controlling to have happy marriages.
Politically charged gender wars are raging all around. A lot of straight women say they’re fed up with men. Doyle is offering a kind of optimism — but doing it controversially.
Many women have found that her approach works. But many others are repulsed by it. I chatted with Doyle to try to understand her thinking.
